summary: PR #2486 added a decompiled lbmemory function, fn_80015184, with an OSAlarm callback prototype/header include, and added address comments to hsd_3B34 prototypes. The author described this as first-time decomp work using objdiff and tools/decomp.py. Bot output showed score improvements for lbmemory and hsd_3B34 plus a small lbmemory .data regression; reviewer approved while noting hsd_3B34 functions had been submitted with higher scores in PR #2488, but lbmemory could still merge.

key_files.role: Added fn_80015184 implementation, local LBMgr struct, assert strings, memcpy chunking logic, callback invocation, and OS alarm scheduling.
Added OSAlarm include and prototype for fn_80015184(OSAlarm*, OSContext*).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbmemory.h hunk adding <dolphin/os/OSAlarm.h> and fn_80015184 prototype

key_files.role: Added address comments to existing hsd_3B34 prototypes.

what_changed.text: Added fn_80015184 in lbmemory.c as an OSAlarm callback-like function taking OSAlarm* and OSContext* parameters.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void fn_80015184(OSAlarm* alarm, OSContext* context)

what_changed.text: Recovered a temporary local struct LBMgr containing an OSAlarm, source/destination pointers, size, offset, callback argument, and callback pointer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct LBMgr fields alarm, src, dst, size, offset, cb_arg, cb

what_changed.text: Function copies up to 0x19000 bytes from src+offset to dst+offset, updates offset, calls cb when complete, otherwise recreates/sets an alarm.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: memcpy and OSCreateAlarm/OSSetAlarm sequence in fn_80015184

what_changed.text: Added static strings for lbmemory.c and p->size and used a raw __assert when size is zero.
Added an OSAlarm include and fn_80015184 prototype to lbmemory.h.
Added address comments to hsd_3B34.h prototypes without changing their signatures.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated the public header alongside the new function body, including the OSAlarm type dependency needed by the recovered signature.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbmemory.h adds <dolphin/os/OSAlarm.h> and fn_80015184 prototype

smart_moves.text: The author used objdiff and tools/decomp.py as stated in the PR body, giving at least some tooling context for the decomp attempt.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says they chose functions using objdiff to decomp with python tools/decomp.py

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering a function body that proves a callback signature, update the owning header instead of leaving the function source-local.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbmemory.h adds fn_80015184(OSAlarm* alarm, OSContext* context)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Score reports can show simultaneous code improvements and data-section regressions; treat data/literal changes as review-sensitive rather than assuming all score movement is positive.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows lbmemory fn_80015184 improvement and lbmemory .sdata improvement
text_corpus: bot report shows main/melee/lb/lbmemory .data regression -3 bytes, 93.33% to 92.18%

decomp_lessons.lesson: If another PR has higher match scores for overlapping functions, reviewers may choose to merge only the non-overlapping useful portion.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: ribbanya review says hsd_3B34 functions were submitted with higher match scores in #2488, but lbmemory can still merge

naming_conventions.text: Unproven function names remained address-style, e.g. fn_80015184 and hsd_803B3408-style names.

The temporary recovered struct was named LBMgr and fields were given role names such as src, dst, size, offset, cb_arg, and cb; the PR evidence does not show whether these names were reviewed as canonical.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a temporary internal struct to replace raw offset accesses within fn_80015184, while still deriving the object from &g_alloc + 0x6A0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct LBMgr and temp_r3 = (struct LBMgr*) ((u8*) &g_alloc + 0x6A0)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Implemented chunk-size capping with an explicit if against 0x19000 before memcpy.

Raw __assert and static assert strings were used in the submitted code; loaded standards would generally route future cleanup toward assert macros and careful literal/data ownership checks, but this PR slice does not include reviewer discussion of that choice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static s8 lbl_803BA2C0, lbMemory_804D3794, and __assert

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Thanks for the contribution; hsd_3B34 functions were already submitted with higher match scores in #2488, but lbmemory could still be merged.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report showed improvements for fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, fn_80015184, hsd_3B34 .sdata2, and lbmemory .sdata, plus a lbmemory .data regression.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01

follow_up_queries: LBMgr struct lbmemory src dst size offset cb_arg cb

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed files, merge state
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for three changed files
text_corpus: PR body, bot report, approving review
notes: Diff and text corpus are sufficient to identify changed files, added code shape, score-report effects, and explicit reviewer feedback.
The PR body says objdiff/tools/decomp.py were used, but no narrow build, objdiff screenshot, or final validation log is included beyond the bot score report.
No inline review comments are present.

The hsd_3B34 header edits are visible, but reviewer feedback says overlapping hsd_3B34 functions had higher scores in PR #2488; this slice does not prove which hsd-related changes, if any, were desirable beyond the merged diff.
Raw assert strings and data-section score movement are observable, but there is no review discussion proving author intent or accepted style for those choices.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Overlapping decomp PRs may merge only the useful non-overlapping unit
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2486, reviewer ribbanya approved while noting hsd_3B34 functions had higher match scores in PR #2488, but lbmemory could still merge. This supports a review pattern of salvaging a useful matched function even when another PR supersedes overlapping work.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Score reports can combine text progress with data regressions
accepted_candidate_records.text: The bot report for PR #2486 showed fn_80015184 improving from 0.00% to 88.61% and lbmemory .sdata improving, while lbmemory .data regressed from 93.33% to 92.18%. Curators may use this as a concrete example when documenting why data-section changes need separate scrutiny.

source_update_candidates.title: lbmemory contains OSAlarm callback candidate fn_80015184
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2486 added fn_80015184 in src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c and declared it in lbmemory.h as void fn_80015184(OSAlarm* alarm, OSContext* context). The body uses a local LBMgr-shaped object at &g_alloc + 0x6A0, copies up to 0x19000 bytes per alarm, invokes a callback when complete, and otherwise calls OSCreateAlarm/OSSetAlarm.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-specific recovered behavior may be useful for future lbmemory curation, but should be verified against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Historical lbmemory data-section regression tied to first fn_80015184 PR
source_update_candidates.text: The PR #2486 bot report recorded lbmemory .data regression while the diff added static assert strings in lbmemory.c. The slice does not prove causality, but it is a useful historical search hook for future investigation of lbmemory literals/data ownership.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static s8 lbl_803BA2C0 and lbMemory_804D3794
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data/literal ownership note; evidence is mixed and should not be promoted without current-source and section metadata review.

rejection_notes: Do not record hsd_3B34 function matching lessons from this PR alone; reviewer said those functions had higher scores in PR #2488, and this slice only shows header address comments.
Do not treat raw __assert plus static string storage as an accepted style from this PR; loaded standards prefer macro/literal ownership scrutiny, and no review comment endorses the raw form.
Do not claim fn_80015184 fully matched; the bot report shows 88.61% after, not a complete match, and no final objdiff validation log is included.
